History Changed By Date Version Notes: Yamini Pulusu 02/ 23 /202 2 0.0 Original document. EX 28 – Replenishment Task Release and Priority Bumping During inventory adjustments or cycle counts, it is possible for users to update the pick location’s on hand quantity to 0 for MCLN. Base supports the release of P50 replenishment tasks only if a user picks a location below what is configured for minimum to release held replenishment. With EX28, WM is modified so that if a pick location’s on hand quantity ever goes to 0 units or packs, regardless of how that update happens , and if there are only locked P50 and P60 replenishments, to release the highest priority replenishment and b ump up to a higher priority that is configur ed able . Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N This extension is supported by a trigger on the WM_INVENTORY table and is called when the pick location’s on hand quantity goes to 0. Assumptions WM releases replenishment tasks only if all of the replenishment tasks available are in locked/disabled status This extension does not come into play if there are any released replenishment tasks available for the item/location combination P60s are also eligible to be released as part of this extension If the only available tasks are locked replenishment tasks with multiple items, WM releases the replenishment task if the on-hand quantity goes to 0 for any one of the items on the replenishment task If a user cycle counts to 0, the count needs to be booked so that the inventory updates occur to invoke this logic This extension does not take into consideration on hand quantities that are negative This extension only looks if the on-hand quantities for locations is brought down to 0 Validations The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension . First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 28 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 28 is turned off, and no custom EX2 8 processing will occur. Lastly, the bump up priority must be configured. This is controlled through custom system code C-E 28 code id 01 byte 2 - 5 . Validations Updates Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Diagram Process Steps UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE Outputs Activity Tracking PIX No changes to base behavior for PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks Not in scope as part of this extension. RF No RF changes in scope for this extension. Process Change/Addition No additional process changes in scope for this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below.
